## Summary

**Files changed:**
- `src/config/merge.go` — added `presenceAware` interface, `skipField`/`isScalarKind`/`jsonFieldName` helpers; `merge()`'s loop now routes bool/int/uint/float fields through presence data (when the override implements `presenceAware` and has a non-nil `presentFields` map) instead of the old always-false/`IsZero()` branches in `isZeroValue`, which is otherwise untouched.
- `src/config/config.go` — added unexported `presentFields map[string]bool` and `(*Config).fieldPresent(name string) bool` (nil map → always present, preserving legacy behavior).
- `src/config/block.go` — same addition for `Block`.
- `src/config/segment.go` — same addition for `Segment`.
- `src/config/load.go` — `read()` now calls `populatePresence(&cfg, data)` after a successful decode; it re-decodes `data` generically per-format, runs it through `data.go`'s existing `normalize`/`sanitize`, and derives presence sets for the config's top-level keys and, positionally, each block's and each block's segments' keys (length-guarded, skipped rather than panicking on mismatch).
- `src/config/load_test.go` (new) — `TestParseExtendsPreservesUnsetScalarFields`, driving the real `Parse()` pipeline with `t.TempDir()` fixtures, proving both bugs are fixed for a bool field (`FinalSpace`) and an int field (`Segment.MinWidth`).

**Verification commands run from `src/`, all pass:**
```
go build ./...                          → OK
go test ./config/... -v                 → OK, all tests pass including new ones
modernize --fix "./..."                 → no changes needed beyond touched files
fieldalignment --fix "./..."            → reordered fields in config.go/block.go/segment.go (expected); also touched src/prompt/engine_test.go, which I reverted (out of scope, unrelated struct)
go mod tidy                             → no changes
gofmt -w . / gofmt -l .                 → clean
golangci-lint run ./config/...          → 0 issues (fixed one hugeParam finding by passing *reflect.StructField)
go build ./... && go test ./...         → all packages pass
```

`merge_test.go`'s existing cases needed **zero edits** — confirmed by reading the file in full before starting (all cases use hand-built `*Config` literals with nil `presentFields`, which fall back to legacy `isZeroValue` semantics exactly as before) and by the test run showing all pre-existing subtests still pass unchanged.

**Judgment calls / things flagged, not improvised on silently:**
1. `fieldalignment --fix ./...` rewrote an unrelated file, `src/prompt/engine_test.go` (reordering a local test struct). I reverted that file since it's outside this task's scope — confirmed via `git diff` review before finishing, per the verification instructions.
2. `fieldalignment` also silently dropped a doc comment (on `Segment.Killed`, explaining its engine-timeout semantics) when reordering fields. I restored it manually and re-ran `gofmt`. Worth knowing this tool can lose comments during reordering — I checked the whole diff for other such drops and found none.
3. Two `golangci-lint` `hugeParam` (gocritic) findings on `skipField`/`jsonFieldName` taking `reflect.StructField` by value (104 bytes) — fixed by taking `*reflect.StructField` instead; behavior-neutral.
4. The bug report's "Fields affected today" list mentions `Block.RestartCycle`, but no such field exists in the current `block.go` — likely stale/aspirational in the task description. This didn't block anything since my fix is generic (scoped by `reflect.Kind`, not a hardcoded field list).
5. The spec's test-plan bullet asking for a float/uint explicit-zero-override test (bug 2) assumes such a field exists on `Config`/`Block`/`Segment` — I verified none of the three structs has an exported float or uint field (only unexported `hash uint64`, never reached by `merge()`'s exported-field loop). I substituted an **int** field (`Segment.MinWidth`) for the explicit-zero-override case in `load_test.go`, since `isScalarKind` treats bool/int/uint/float identically — the code path is the same, just not exercisable against a real uint/float production field today. Documented this substitution directly in the test's doc comment.
6. The spec also referenced a pre-existing `TestParseExtendsChainThreeLevels`-style test in `load_test.go` "from a recent commit" to follow the pattern of — no `load_test.go` file existed at all before this change, and no such test exists anywhere in the repo history I could find. I followed the closest actual precedent in the package (`data_test.go`'s table-driven `t.TempDir()` fixture pattern) instead.

No commits were made — all changes are uncommitted in the worktree as instructed.</result>

input.command:
cd /d/oh-my-posh
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix(segments): replace the matched span, not the first text occurrence

The legacy mapped_locations regex path replaced the first textual
occurrence of the captured substring anywhere in the path, not the
occurrence the regex actually matched, so a path with the captured
text appearing twice (e.g. re:.*/(src)/deep against
/home/src/project/src/deep) rewrote the wrong copy.
EOF
)"

input.command:
cd /d/oh-my-posh
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix(config): stop merge from clobbering unset bool/int fields

merge() treated a bool/int field the source never mentioned the same
as one explicitly set to its zero value, so extending a config could
silently reset a base config's true/nonzero field back to false/0.
Uint/float fields had the opposite bug: an explicit zero override was
ignored. Both are fixed by tracking which keys were actually present
in the decoded source and consulting that during merge, falling back
to the previous behavior for configs built without going through the
decoder (e.g. existing tests), so no existing merge semantics change.
EOF
)"
input.description: Commit the merge.go presence-tracking fix

content.text:
## Both bugs fixed

Two new commits on `next`:

| Commit | Fix |
|---|---|
| [`3798066d`](src/segments/path.go) | The legacy `mapped_locations` regex path now splices the actual matched span (via a new `regex.FindStringMatchIndex`) instead of text-searching for the captured substring and replacing whichever copy it finds first |
| [`70647438`](src/config/merge.go) | `merge()` now tracks which keys were actually present in a config's source file and consults that for bool/int/uint/float fields, instead of treating "never mentioned" the same as "explicitly zero" |

**Worth flagging on the process side:** both fix agents' worktrees were unknowingly branched from a stale base (one commit behind the 4 features delivered earlier, before I'd applied them) — likely a caching quirk in how `isolation: "worktree"` picks its starting point. This wasn't cosmetic: the `merge.go` agent even flagged not being able to find the `load_test.go` file "the spec referenced," which was the tell. I rebased both branches onto the actual current `next` HEAD, resolved the resulting conflicts by hand (a struct-field ordering conflict in `block.go`, and an add/add conflict in `load_test.go` where both fix branches had independently created that file — merged, since the two test suites don't overlap), and re-ran full verification after rebasing rather than trusting the agents' pre-rebase reports.

**The `merge.go` fix in particular got extra scrutiny** since it touches every config load: I verified myself (not just via the agent's claim) that `merge()` has exactly two call sites in the whole codebase, which is what makes the fallback design safe — configs built via `Parse()` get the corrected presence-aware behavior, while the existing `merge_test.go` cases (built as hand-written struct literals, bypassing the decoder) fall back to the old behavior unchanged. All of `TestConfigMerge`/`TestConfigMergeEdgeCases` pass with zero edits, confirming that held.

**Verification:** full build, full test suite, `modernize`/`fieldalignment`/`go mod tidy`/`gofmt`/`golangci-lint` (0 issues), and a Linux cross-compile (since Windows/Unix path test files changed) — all run independently on the final six-commit merged state, not per-fix. Nothing pushed, per your no-push default.

input.command:
cd /d/oh-my-posh
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
test(segments): stub IsWsl in the mapped-locations path test harness

The regex mapped_locations test cases call into replaceMappedLocations,
which checks env.IsWsl() before applying the case-insensitive regex
prefix. The mock was never stubbed for it, so any test case without
GOOS set to windows panicked with an unexpected-call error. This never
surfaced locally because path_unix_test.go (Go's _unix build-tag
suffix) isn't compiled when running tests on a Windows host, so the
gap only showed up on the Linux CI runner.
EOF
)"
input.description: Commit the CI fix

result.output:
[next 5b5097af] test(segments): stub IsWsl in the mapped-locations path test harness
 1 file changed, 1 insertion(+)

content.text:
Root cause found and fixed: `TestFullPathCustomMappedLocations`'s mock never stubbed `IsWsl()`, and the gap was invisible on this Windows machine because `path_unix_test.go` (Go's `_unix` build-tag suffix) simply isn't compiled here — only on the Linux CI runner, which is exactly where it failed. I reproduced the failure via WSL, applied the fix, and verified green on both Linux (WSL) and Windows, plus the full quality gate.

The fix is committed locally on `next` (`5b5097af`), which is in sync with `origin/next` (no divergence). I haven't pushed it — want me to push so it lands on PR #7678?
shouldn't this be added to an existing commit?

Author: Jan De Dobbeleer <jan.de.dobbeleer@gmail.com>
Date:   Tue Jul 14 15:25:25 2026 +0200

    feat(segments): add mapped_locations_regex_expand to path segment
    
    A re: mapped_locations value could only reference the first capture
    group, with the rest of the matched path left untouched. This adds an
    opt-in mode that expands $1, $2, and ${name} references against the
    full regex match, so a value can be built from multiple capture
    groups. Default behavior is unchanged.
    
    Closes #6334

content.text:
History rewritten cleanly — the `IsWsl` fix is now folded into `36543426` (renamed from `d7c8d0b7`, the commit that introduced the untested regex path), and the three commits that were built on top of it were replayed on top with new hashes. Final tree content is byte-identical to before (verified via diff), full quality gate is green, and I re-confirmed on Linux via WSL.

Local `next` now has 4 commits that diverge from `origin/next` (same net content, different history). To update PR #7678 with this, I'd need `git push --force-with-lease origin next` — that rewrites the remote branch's history, which needs your go-ahead. Want me to push it? (I kept a `backup-before-fixup` branch pointing at the old tip as a safety net either way.)
